requirements: Snap-in dorsal electronics and battery module

verify.value: Motor power lines and sensor/encoder signal lines run in separate channels.
verify.rule: [power_signal_separation] good: Motor power lines and sensor/encoder signal lines run in separate channels or are spaced/crossed at right angles, with the separation visible in routing geometry. | bad: Power and signal wires share one tight bundle along the whole route with no separation or shielding note.

verify.value: Each electronics module states its grounding scheme (star point, chassis bond, isolated) and signal returns route alongside their signals.
verify.rule: [return_path_and_grounding_intent] good: Each electronics module states its grounding scheme (star point, chassis bond, isolated) and signal returns route alongside their signals. | bad: Grounding is unmentioned; returns are implied through structure with no bond feature or note.

verify.value: PCBs mount on standoffs with keep-out clearance to walls and moving parts, connector edges face access openings, and mounting holes match standard board patterns.
verify.rule: [board_mounting_and_clearance] good: PCBs mount on standoffs with keep-out clearance to walls and moving parts, connector edges face access openings, and mounting holes match standard board patterns. | bad: Boards are floated or glued in cavities with no standoffs, clearance, or connector orientation logic.

risk.desc: Insufficient shielding of sensitive electronics from external EMI.
risk.mit: Implement Faraday cage enclosure around controller board.

risk.desc: Connector durability in repeated mating cycles.
risk.mit: Select connectors with high cycle life and secure locking mechanisms.

generated_parts.label: Battery + Power Module x 2
generated_parts.spec: Wrist battery pocket with quick-swap door, li-ion pack 2S 18650, BMS access window, power connector keying, charge LED window

verify.value: Battery module is removable via quick-swap door
verify.rule: [replaceable_module_path] good: Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown. | bad: Functional modules are fused into a closed body.

verify.value: Cable routing channels are visible and accessible
verify.rule: [cable_harness_visibility] good: Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels. | bad: Cables are buried inside closed links or shells with no inspection feature.

verify.value: Quick-swap door provides physical access to battery compartment
verify.rule: [access_not_just_text] good: Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover. | bad: Access is claimed only in prose.

verify.value: Strain relief features are incorporated at cable exits
verify.rule: [harness_strain_relief_and_bend_radius] good: Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges. | bad: Cables exit bare holes, cross hinge lines unsupported, or bend around sharp printed edges with no relief feature.

verify.value: Power connector is accessible and keyed for proper mating
verify.rule: [connector_accessibility_and_keying] good: Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating. | bad: Connectors face closed walls, sit in unreachable pockets, or identical adjacent connectors have no keying or label.

risk.desc: BMS access window may be susceptible to dust ingress
risk.mit: Implement a gasket seal around the window
Use threadlocker on critical fasteners

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Battery pocket at wrist with quick-swap access
physics_paths: electrical/data: Battery to BMS and power connector
thermal: Convection through access window
mechanical: Mounting forces transmitted through wrist band

generated_parts.label: Wiring Loom + Service Loop Set
generated_parts.spec: Knuckle-crossing wiring loom with service loops, strain relief at every flex boundary, flex-rated bend radius, power vs signal channel separation

requirements: Knuckle-crossing wiring loom with service loops, strain relief at every flex boundary, flex-rated bend radius, power vs signal channel separation

verify.value: Battery, sensor, actuator cartridges are removable
verify.rule: [replaceable_module_path] good: Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown. | bad: Functional modules are fused into a closed body.

verify.value: Cables routed through visible channels
verify.rule: [cable_harness_visibility] good: Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels. | bad: Cables are buried inside closed links or shells with no inspection feature.

verify.value: Access hatches provided for connectors
verify.rule: [access_not_just_text] good: Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover. | bad: Access is claimed only in prose.

verify.value: Strain relief boots at flex points
verify.rule: [harness_strain_relief_and_bend_radius] good: Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges. | bad: Cables exit bare holes, cross hinge lines unsupported, or bend around sharp printed edges with no relief feature.

verify.value: Connectors accessible with clearance
verify.rule: [connector_accessibility_and_keying] good: Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating. | bad: Connectors face closed walls, sit in unreachable pockets, or identical adjacent connectors have no keying or label.

verify.value: Cartridges removable via hinge
verify.rule: [finger_cartridge_service] good: Per-finger linkage cartridges are removable by single pin or visible hinge access. | bad: Finger linkages are glued, buried, or represented only as a glove shell.

verify.value: Actuators plug in and remain visible
verify.rule: [actuator_module_access] good: Flexion-resistance actuator modules plug in and remain visible near knuckle row. | bad: Actuators are bonded or hidden without connector/service geometry.

verify.value: Rigid mock assembly preserves soft contact cues
verify.rule: [wearable_comfort_boundary] good: Rigid mock assembly preserves soft-contact cues, wrist quick release, and ergonomic clearance. | bad: Rigid shell ignores don/doff, comfort, and finger clearance.

verify.value: Service loops at flex points
verify.rule: [flex_cycle_harness_durability] good: Finger and wrist wiring uses service loops, strain relief at every flex boundary, and a stated bend radius sized for repeated flexion cycles. | bad: Wires run taut across knuckles or terminate at rigid mounts with no relief, inviting fatigue failure.

verify.value: Power and signal lines separated
verify.rule: [power_signal_separation] good: Motor power lines and sensor/encoder signal lines run in separate channels or are spaced/crossed at right angles, with the separation visible in routing geometry. | bad: Power and signal wires share one tight bundle along the whole route with no separation or shielding note.

verify.value: Grounding scheme documented
verify.rule: [return_path_and_grounding_intent] good: Each electronics module states its grounding scheme (star point, chassis bond, isolated) and signal returns route alongside their signals. | bad: Grounding is unmentioned; returns are implied through structure with no bond feature or note.

verify.value: PCBs mounted on standoffs
verify.rule: [board_mounting_and_clearance] good: PCBs mount on standoffs with keep-out clearance to walls and moving parts, connector edges face access openings, and mounting holes match standard board patterns. | bad: Boards are floated or glued in cavities with no standoffs, clearance, or connector orientation logic.

verify.value: Connector mates specified
verify.rule: [connection_type_named] good: Every inter-part interface names a specific connection type (bolted, threaded_insert, snap_fit, press_fit, dovetail_slide, clevis_pin, revolute_bearing, band_clamp, adhesive_lap, o_ring, connector_mate, harness_passthrough) with its DOF and a numeric clearance. | bad: Interfaces are labeled only 'mount' or 'attach' with no connection type, DOF, or clearance.

verify.value: Assembly sequence documented
verify.rule: [assembly_sequence_feasible] good: Parts can be installed in a stated order where every fastener, connector, and mating feature stays reachable; no installed part blocks access to a later one; last-installed and captive parts are identified. | bad: Assembly order is unstated or impossible — a part traps another, or a fastener ends up behind a wall with no access.

verify.value: Datum chain established
verify.rule: [datum_chain_continuity] good: One primary datum part anchors the assembly; each other part locates off a named parent datum using a 3-2-1 scheme, so tolerance does not accumulate uncontrolled across the stack. | bad: Parts float with no shared datum, or each part references a different feature so stack-up grows part-to-part.

verify.value: Load path traced to ground
verify.rule: [load_path_closure] good: Every part's primary load traces through named interfaces to the base/ground; the load path closes for all parts and high-load joints use load-appropriate connection types. | bad: One or more parts carry load with no continuous path to ground, or a high-load joint uses a low-load connection (e.g. snap-fit holding a structural member).

verify.value: Design moves in the right direction
verify.rule: [structural_development_direction] good: Compared to the previous version the design moves the right direction: fewer interfaces, parts consolidated where service is preserved, open load paths closed, and redundancy added only at critical joints. | bad: Iteration adds interfaces or loose parts without justification, or consolidates in a way that buries service access.

verify.value: Interfaces minimized
verify.rule: [interface_minimization] good: The assembly uses the fewest interfaces that still allow service and manufacture; each remaining interface is justified by service, motion, manufacture split, or load transfer. | bad: Many redundant or unexplained interfaces, or arbitrary part splits that add joints without a service/manufacture reason.

verify.value: Mass estimate within budget
verify.rule: cad_brief.mass_est_g must be a numeric grams value, and verify must include a mass/budget check.

risk.mit: Use strain relief boots with appropriate bend radius
Ensure connector alignment features are precise and robust
Secure cartridges with reliable locking mechanism
